headlines.description: Brentford's Kevin Schade bagged a hat-trick, plus set up a fourth goal, as Thomas Frank's men beat Leicester City 4-1 on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Van Nistelrooy watches on as Leicester thrashed

headlines.description: A header deep into second-half stoppage time from midfielder Daniel Muñoz gave Crystal Palace a much-needed draw against Newcastle on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Muñoz goal earns Palace draw at Newcastle

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest's New Zealand striker Chris Wood's second-half penalty downed Ipswich Town 1-0 at the City Ground on Saturday as he became the club's joint-top Premier League scorer.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wood equals Forest scoring record in Ipswich win

headlines.description: Dutchman Justin Kluivert became the first player in Premier League history to score a hat-trick of penalties in a single match as Bournemouth beat Wolverhampton Wanderers 4-2 at the Molineux Stadium on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Kluivert nets three pens to hand Bournemouth win

headlines.description: A resurgent Arsenal team romped to a 5-2 victory at West Ham United on Saturday, with all seven goals coming in a dramatic first half in east London.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal thrash West Ham after 7-goal first half
